settings.title: Service Designer Skill Matrix
settings.subtitle: Mark the skills you identify with or have experience in
settings.instructions: Click on any skill segment to mark it. Click again to unmark.

core.name: Service blueprinting
core.explanation: Creating detailed visualizations of service processes, showing frontstage/backstage activities, touchpoints, and support systems.

core.name: Customer journey mapping
core.explanation: Mapping end-to-end customer experiences across channels, identifying pain points, emotions, and opportunities.

core.name: User research & interviews
core.explanation: Planning and conducting qualitative research to understand user needs, behaviors, and motivations.

core.name: Stakeholder mapping
core.explanation: Identifying and visualizing relationships between all parties involved in or affected by a service.

core.name: Workshop design & facilitation
core.explanation: Designing and leading collaborative sessions to align teams, generate ideas, and make decisions.

core.name: Service prototyping
core.explanation: Creating low-fidelity simulations of service experiences to test and iterate on concepts.

core.name: Systems thinking
core.explanation: Understanding interconnections, feedback loops, and emergent behaviors within complex service ecosystems.

core.name: Touchpoint design
core.explanation: Designing individual interaction points where customers engage with the service across channels.

core.name: Co-design
core.explanation: Involving users, stakeholders, and frontline staff as active participants in the design process.

core.name: Research synthesis
core.explanation: Analyzing and distilling research findings into actionable insights, patterns, and opportunities.

core.name: Business model understanding
core.explanation: Grasping how services create, deliver, and capture value within organizational constraints.

core.name: Process mapping
core.explanation: Documenting and analyzing operational workflows to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities.

supporting.name: Storytelling & narrative
supporting.explanation: Crafting compelling stories to communicate insights, concepts, and the value of design decisions.

supporting.name: Empathy
supporting.explanation: Deeply understanding and sharing the feelings of users, customers, and stakeholders.

supporting.name: Cross-functional collaboration
supporting.explanation: Working effectively across disciplines, departments, and organizational boundaries.

supporting.name: Strategic communication
supporting.explanation: Tailoring messages to different audiences and contexts to drive understanding and action.

supporting.name: Influencing without authority
supporting.explanation: Building buy-in and driving change without formal power or hierarchical position.

supporting.name: Problem framing
supporting.explanation: Defining and reframing challenges to ensure teams are solving the right problems.

supporting.name: Adaptability
supporting.explanation: Adjusting approach and methods based on context, constraints, and emerging information.

supporting.name: Managing ambiguity
supporting.explanation: Staying effective and guiding others when goals, processes, or outcomes are unclear.

supporting.name: Stakeholder management
supporting.explanation: Building relationships, managing expectations, and navigating organizational politics.

supporting.name: Visual communication
supporting.explanation: Using diagrams, sketches, and visual artifacts to convey complex ideas clearly.
